General External Cause Coding Guidelines.never_principal: External cause codes are never sequenced as first-listed/principal diagnosis.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.purpose: Used to capture cause, intent, place, activity, and status for injury/condition for injury research and prevention strategies.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.mandatory_reporting: No national requirement; may be required by state or payer. Voluntary reporting encouraged.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.usage_scope: May be used with any code in A00.0–T88.9, Z00–Z99 representing health condition due to external cause (e.g., infections from external sources, MI during strenuous activity).

length_of_treatment.rule: Assign external cause code with matching 7th character (A/D/S) for each encounter while condition is treated.
length_of_treatment.matching: Match 7th character of external cause code to 7th character of associated injury/condition code.

General External Cause Coding Guidelines.full_range: Use the full range of codes to describe cause, intent, place, activity, and status.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.multiple_codes: Assign as many codes as needed to explain each cause; if only one possible, choose the one most related to principal diagnosis.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.selection: Guided by Alphabetic Index of External Causes and Tabular List notes.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.combination_codes: Some external cause codes identify sequential events (e.g., fall then striking object); use code matching sequence regardless of severity.
General External Cause Coding Guidelines.no_code_needed: No Chapter 20 external cause code needed if cause and intent included in another chapter's code (e.g., T36.0X1-).

Place of Occurrence Guideline.usage: Secondary code used after other external cause codes to identify location at time of event.
Place of Occurrence Guideline.once_per_encounter: Assigned once at initial encounter, unless new injury occurs during hospitalization.
Place of Occurrence Guideline.no_7th_char: Y92 codes do not use 7th characters.
Place of Occurrence Guideline.avoid_unspecified: Do not use Y92.9 if place is not stated or not applicable.

Activity Code Guideline.usage: Describes activity at time of event; assign once at initial encounter only.
Activity Code Guideline.not_for: Not used for poisonings, adverse effects, misadventures, or sequela.
Activity Code Guideline.avoid_unspecified: Do not assign Y93.9 if activity not stated.
Activity Code Guideline.additional_info: May be used with cause/intent codes if adds useful detail.

Place, Activity, and Status Sequencing.sequence: When applicable, sequence after main external cause code(s).
Place, Activity, and Status Sequencing.limit: Generally one place, one activity, one status code per encounter, except when new injury occurs during hospitalization.

If Reporting Format Limits Codes.priority: Report cause/intent most related to principal diagnosis first.
If Reporting Format Limits Codes.extra_capacity: If space allows, include causes/intents of additional events before place/activity/status codes.

Multiple External Cause Coding Guideline.requirement: Use more than one code if needed to fully describe cause of illness/injury.
Multiple External Cause Coding Guideline.first_listed: Should correspond to cause of most serious diagnosis, following hierarchy.

Child and Adult Abuse Guideline.perpetrator_code: If perpetrator known, add Y07 code.

Unknown or Undetermined Intent Guideline.default: If intent unknown/unspecified, default to accidental.
Unknown or Undetermined Intent Guideline.transport_accidents: Assume accidental intent for all transport accident categories.
Unknown or Undetermined Intent Guideline.undetermined_intent_use: Use only if documentation specifies that intent cannot be determined.

Sequelae (Late Effects) of External Caus.sequela_codes: Report with 7th character 'S' for late effects; use with any late effect of a prior injury.
Sequelae (Late Effects) of External Caus.no_current_injury: Never use with related current injury code.
Sequelae (Late Effects) of External Caus.subsequent_visits: Use late effect code for visits treating late effects; do not use for follow-up/rehab when no late effect exists.

confirmed_by_FBI.usage: If FBI identifies as terrorism, first-list Y38 code.
confirmed_by_FBI.place: Add Y92 for place of occurrence.
confirmed_by_FBI.multiple: Assign multiple Y38 codes if more than one mechanism.

Terrorism Guidelines.suspected: If suspected but not confirmed, code as assault.

secondary_effects.code: Y38.9 for conditions occurring after terrorist event.
secondary_effects.exclusion: Do not assign for conditions due to initial act.
secondary_effects.combination: May be used with another Y38 code if both initial and subsequent effects exist.

External Cause Status.usage: Assign with any external cause code (including activity) to indicate work/military/non-work status at event time.
External Cause Status.applicable_to: Applicable with transport accidents, falls, etc.
External Cause Status.not_applicable_to: Not used for poisonings, adverse effects, misadventures, or late effects.
External Cause Status.avoid_unspecified: Do not use Y99.9 if status not stated.
